heroTitle: Peer-validated experts who assess the grants.
heroDek: A curated, peer-attested collective of practitioners advancing the intersection of localism and Ethereum-based coordination. They assess, advise, and amplify the local hubs the Fund backs — so every grant benefits from both high-level expertise and grounded community context.

what.lede: Not a panel of distant judges. A network of people who've run the rounds, built the tools, and done the local work.
paragraphs: The Expert Network is how the Fund stays credible. Experts reach Validated status through peer attestation — vouched for by others in the network via TrustGraph, not appointed from above — and bring proven experience across one or more of three domains.

For Round 01, the network was the engine of evaluation: experts scored every local-hub application against the rubric, advised hubs as they designed their programs, and surfaced the localism and on-chain signal that the operators built funding decisions on.

items.name: Grant-making
items.body: Designing, managing, or evaluating grant programs — across Web3, philanthropy, civic, and public sectors.

items.name: Web3 / Ethereum tooling
items.body: Fluency with Ethereum tools for coordination, funding, governance, and identity — Karma GAP, Gardens, Giveth, Allo, Prosperity Pass.

items.name: Localism
items.body: Hands-on experience building or advising initiatives across political, economic, cultural, ecological, or Ethereum localism.

roster.lede: The network is a public database — practitioners who applied, were peer-attested, and vouched for one another via TrustGraph. These are the people behind Round 01.
roster.note: From the public Expert Network database. Round 01 contributors were selected from this group; the network isn't open for new applications right now.

round01.lede: Round 01 was the network's first live use case. Validated experts contributed across three areas — pairing 3–4 reviewers per application, balanced for geography, language, and expertise.

roles.name: Evaluation & intelligence
roles.body: Score every application against the rubric and add structured qualitative feedback, surfacing the credible, high-potential programs.

roles.name: Mentorship & advisory
roles.body: Pair 1–3 experts with each selected hub for hands-on help on governance, tooling, grant operations, and impact reporting.

roles.name: Tooling feedback
roles.body: Stress-test the Web3 tools in real use and feed back what should change for the next round.

round01.matching: Scores set the match: up to 2× a hub's local co-funding, capped at $20k per grantee.
round01.compensation: $5,500 USD + 10,000 CELO was allocated for expert compensation across contribution tiers.

lifecycle.lede: Experts move through a five-step engagement lifecycle each cycle.

steps.title: Join the network
steps.body: Complete onboarding, give and receive peer attestations, and reach Validated Expert status via TrustGraph.

steps.title: Tier assignment
steps.body: Operators confirm experts for the cycle and suggest a provisional contribution tier.

steps.title: Active contribution
steps.body: Evaluate and score local-hub applications against the official rubric; advise hubs and give tooling feedback.

steps.title: Self-assessment & compensation
steps.body: Experts reassess their contribution tier; compensation flows via Prosperity Pass.

steps.title: Reflection & re-attestation
steps.body: Reflect on the cycle and refresh attestations, carrying the network into the next round.

items.name: Monty Merlin Bryant
items.bio: Regenerative finance practitioner, community builder, and systems entrepreneur working at the intersection of Web3, AI, nature finance, and economic transformation.

items.name: Benjamin Life
items.bio: Civic innovator, researcher, and artist working at the intersection of regenerative crypto-economics, bioregional coordination, and participatory systems design.

items.name: Patricia Parkinson
items.bio: Participation systems architect, ontological designer, and civic innovator with over twenty years leading development programs across Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands.

operators.blurb: Supported by the peer-attested expert network, operators steward the flow of resources through local hubs while keeping the process transparent and accountable.

responsibilities.name: Network coordination
responsibilities.body: Design funding architectures around subsidiarity; connect and weave local hubs across geographies.

responsibilities.name: Evaluation & allocation
responsibilities.body: Work with the expert network to assess programs and direct matching funds where they'll do the most good.

responsibilities.name: Communication & transparency
responsibilities.body: Keep clear channels with applicants, experts, and partners; publish public documentation.

responsibilities.name: Resource stewardship
responsibilities.body: Manage the disbursement of matching funds and track the progress of supported programs.

responsibilities.name: Learning & adaptation
responsibilities.body: Synthesise attestations and learnings across the network into better future rounds.

join.lede: The network isn't open for applications right now.
join.body: Round 01 is complete, and we're not actively onboarding new experts at the moment. If you've done this work and would like to be considered for a future cycle, get in touch.

eligibility.lede: To join, applicants meet three criteria and complete peer validation via TrustGraph:
items: Demonstrated expertise in at least one of grant-making, Web3 / Ethereum tooling, or localism.
Commitment to the OpenCivics Ethical Standards — good-faith collaboration, honesty, accountability.
Intent to contribute constructively — through evaluation, mentorship, feedback, or knowledge-sharing.

faq.a: An on-chain attestation network that maps relationships of trust and expertise. Each expert can attest to others; a dynamic TrustScore — powered by a PageRank-style algorithm — determines when a member becomes a Validated Expert. Credibility is earned peer-to-peer, not assigned top-down.

faq.q: How do I become a validated expert?
faq.a: Fill out the onboarding form, receive attestations from existing members via TrustGraph, and once your TrustScore crosses the validation threshold you're recognised as a Validated Expert — eligible to contribute to active rounds.
Yes. Round 01 had a pool of $5,500 USD + 10,000 CELO, distributed by contribution tier — roughly $200–$1,200 plus on-chain badges, reflecting time, quality, and impact.
Flexible. Experts choose their level — from light engagement (~3–6 hours) to deep mentorship and evaluation (~12–20+ hours) during each active cycle.

A smart-account wallet and reputation layer in the Ethereum and Celo ecosystems. Validated Experts receive a Localism Fund Expert Badge they can claim via Prosperity Pass — proof of contribution, a portable credential across future rounds, and a gateway to rewards and governance.

To evolve into a standing guild of evaluators and mentors serving multiple rounds and partners — an open, interlinked expert commons spanning bioregional governance, ecological restoration & climate finance, cultural regeneration, civic tech, and on-chain coordination.

meta.source: Pulled live from the Localism Fund Notion (Expert Network Public Portal), 2026-06.
